weather.summary: Saturday: 15°C, clear sky. Sunday: 14°C, clear sky

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting opportunities for your little one! From free festivals in Trafalgar Square to puppet shows in Islington and outdoor adventures in Victoria Park, there's something for everyone. With clear skies and mild temperatures, it's the perfect time to explore all that London has to offer.

activities.title: Victoria Park Easter Activities
activities.description: Victoria Park is hosting a fortnight of free, daily activities, including sports, fishing, and pond dipping.
activities.whyItWorks: With diverse activities like sports and pond dipping, there's something for every child. Plenty of space for running around and exploring.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes 277, 425, or a short walk from Hackney Wick Overground.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for a detailed schedule and booking info.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: The Young V&A is a museum dedicated to inspiring children through art, design, and creativity, with interactive galleries and hands-on exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children with engaging exhibits, a play area, and space to be creative. The 'Japan: Designing Nature' exhibit offers sensory experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the construction and design areas for hands-on fun.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Explore London's history as a port city with interactive exhibits and child-friendly activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of effort put into designing it with children in mind. There are free activity sheets specially designed for different ages, and so many hands-on activities for them to enjoy as they explore. They even have special red signs explaining things in an easy-to-read, child-friendly way.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Don't miss Mudlarks, the free soft play area.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with animals, gardens, and educational activities.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see farm animals up close, participate in gardening activities, and learn about where food comes from. Great for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes or a short walk from Bethnal Green tube station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Olympic Park Playgrounds
activities.description: Explore the various playgrounds and green spaces in the Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run, play, and explore different playground structures. The Tumbling Bay playground is especially popular.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the canal.

activities.title: Little Angel Theatre
activities.description: Enjoy puppet shows adapted from Julia Donaldson's picture books, running about 45-50 minutes.
activities.whyItWorks: Shows are short and engaging, perfect for toddlers with shorter attention spans. The intimate space ensures your toddler won't feel lost in a sea of strangers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as shows are popular.

activities.title: Hackney Marshes
activities.description: Explore the open green space, perfect for a walk, picnic, or bike ride.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space for kids to run around and burn energy. Great for a simple, free day out in nature.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes or a walk from Hackney Central.
transport.tip: Bring a kite or ball for added fun.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed playground for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Specially designed for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Located close to the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur skeletons, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic family destination with dinosaurs and interactive exhibits that will captivate any child. The dinosaur-themed hunts are engaging.

transport.fromHackney: District or Circle Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and experiment demonstrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits make learning about science fun and engaging. The Wonderlab offers a great hands-on experience with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities and educational play zones (ticketed).

transport.fromHackney: District or Circle Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance for a hands-on experience.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Family Activities
activities.description: Enjoy a range of family-friendly activities, including free events, workshops, performances, and the Giant Chalkboard.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities to suit different interests, from music and storytelling to art and creative workshops. The Giant Chalkboard is a great way to unleash creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Waterloo or Northern Line to Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for a detailed schedule of events and booking information.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath, home to rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's completely free to enter, and it's home to rare species of birds and animals. They've got wallabies, lemurs, Scottish wildcat and more!

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a bus or walk.
transport.tip: Combine the zoo visit with a picnic in Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: British Library Family Activities
activities.description: Visit the free Fairy Tales exhibition and participate in family activities.
activities.whyItWorks: The Fairy Tales exhibition feels like a proper exhibition and not just a few bonus bits in a corner. There is a dedicated family desk where you can pick up activity trails, choose a 'museum mission', or for little ones a museum backpack, to help you explore.

transport.fromHackney: Victoria Line to Euston.
transport.tip: Check the British Library website for activity times and details.

activities.title: Vaisakhi on the Square
activities.description: Celebrate Sikh culture with music, dance, and food.
activities.whyItWorks: A vibrant cultural experience with lots to see and hear. Children will enjoy the music and colorful performances.

transport.fromHackney: Various tube lines to Charing Cross or Leicester Square.
transport.tip: Expect large crowds and plan accordingly.

activities.title: St George's Day
activities.description: Celebrate English culture with music, dance, and food.
activities.whyItWorks: A lively cultural event with entertainment for all ages. A great way to introduce children to English traditions.

transport.fromHackney: Various tube lines to Charing Cross or Leicester Square.
transport.tip: Check the event schedule for specific activities.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: Step into the world of Paddington Bear in this delightful musical adaptation.
activities.whyItWorks: A heartwarming story with catchy songs and charming characters. A good introduction to musical theatre for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Various tube lines to Charing Cross or Embankment.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance for best availability.

activities.title: Unicorn Theatre Sensory Performances
activities.description: Look out for sensory performances, offering gentle introductions to live theatre without any pressure to sit still or be quiet.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory performances are designed for babies.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge or Tower Hill
transport.tip: Check the Unicorn Theatre website for shows and schedule

activities.title: Regent's Park Open Air Theatre
activities.address: Inner Circle, Regent's Park, London NW1 4NU
activities.description: Enjoy the world premiere of Sherlock Holmes (2 May to 6 June) in Regent's Park Open Air Theatre
activities.whyItWorks: The chase through London begins as Holmes and Watson set out to uncover the mastermind behind a deadly conspiracy.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Baker Street
transport.tip: Check the Regent's Park Open Air Theatre website for show times and schedule

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with a fun Easter activity. A great way to spend a day outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Camden Town or Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online to save money.

activities.title: The Postal Museum
activities.description: Fun, fascinating and brilliantly hands on, The Postal Museum is a total winner for families.
activities.whyItWorks: The exhibits are clever.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Farringdon, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for ages 3-12, it's a perfect day out for the family. Join in the fun as you hunt for the coveted Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues while exploring Henry VIII's gardens and historic surrounds.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Egg'splorers
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter Egg'splorers event returns to Hobbledown Heath from 28th March – 12th April 2026 for a family Easter adventure in West London.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hounslow, then bus
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Fun for all the family celebrating 50 years of Aardman Wallace & Gromit, Shaun the Sheep, Timmy Time, Morph, Creature Comforts and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Celebrating 50 years of Aardman Wallace & Gromit

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Sloane Square
transport.tip: Check the Cadogan Hall website for show times and schedule
